macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices fall as investors weigh mixed signals on Iran peace talks ahead of a ceasefire deadline, reducing the geopolitical risk premium.
primary_drivers: Lower crude price removes geopolitical risk premium
Reduced Strait of Hormuz disruption risk lowers shipping/insurance premia
Compressed refining and E&P margins on lower oil

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of airline operating costs; Delta has highest unhedged fuel exposure among legacies, so lower crude directly expands margins.
Similar unhedged jet fuel exposure; United's international long-haul routes (larger fuel burn per ASM) amplify the benefit of lower crude.
Southwest historically hedges but remaining uncovered portion still benefits; airline peer rotation trade on oil declines.
Fuel is a major variable cost for the express/ground network; lower crude reduces surcharges drag and improves operating leverage.
Bunker fuel is a top-3 cost for cruise operators; lower oil plus easing Middle East risk also reduces itinerary disruption concerns.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with direct upstream earnings leverage to crude; consensus EPS sensitivity is meaningful per $5/bbl move.
Similar upstream leverage; also holds Middle East/Kazakhstan assets where a de-escalation removes the scarcity bid.
Permian pure-play E&P with high torque to WTI; lower oil directly compresses cash flow and buyback pace.
Smaller-cap E&P with high beta to crude prices; peace-talk optimism accelerates selloff in higher-beta energy names.
Oilfield services revenue tracks E&P capex budgets which are set off strip prices; sustained lower oil pressures 2026 activity guidance.
Subsea/offshore equipment backlog partially tied to Middle East projects; reduced regional tension cools urgency for capacity additions.
Product tanker rates have carried a Strait of Hormuz/Red Sea risk premium; ceasefire progress compresses tanker day rates.
Crude tanker (VLCC) spot rates are levered to Middle East disruption premia; de-escalation removes the tailwind that's supported 2025-26 rates.

headlines: Oil falls as investors assess mixed messaging on Iran peace talks ahead of ceasefire deadline
